content_en:
The scene unfolded on a dark river, atop a small ferry. A violet lamp stood still, shining alone, but its light barely spread beyond its immediate vicinity. It was just enough to see one’s own hands.

Despite being a familiar setting, today it felt more eerie than usual. Even the ferryman's tone was heavier, resonating through the heart and shaking the mind.

Encrid, intrigued by a particularly interesting part of what was being said, asked.

"A path?"

Wasn’t he supposed to enjoy the suffering caused by being trapped against a wall? So why give advice?

"Step back and watch. If you evade, the path will open."

Usually, today was a day to endure and start anew. In other words, evading wouldn't be a way to face tomorrow.

But now, he was being told to avoid it?

The ferryman’s voice grew even more weighty.

"Evade."

The whining voice pierced through Encrid’s entire being, as if it was tearing through his insides.

There was no pain, it was a matter of sensation. No, this was the realm of the mind, so it wasn’t even a sensation. It was a mental issue.

What mattered was not the effect the voice had, but something deeper.

Encrid did not doubt the ferryman. He didn’t question his purpose either. The ferryman’s role was to keep him in ‘today’.

Perhaps that’s why he understood.

"A demon always comes in the guise of an angel."

Suddenly, a line from the sacred texts Audin recited every day came to mind.

"Evade."

The ferryman, still tearing through Encrid’s insides and scrambling his thoughts, continued to speak.

With that, the dark river suddenly receded, and Encrid found himself opening his eyes—again—a strange experience, as if he had already opened them once before, and he faced a new day.

The ferryman’s words lingered in his mind more vividly than ever. Unlike usual dreams, which faded into a vague memory, this one did not. It felt as if he had been brainwashed.

'Run away. Turn back. Abandon just one child, and you can easily pass this day. That would be enough.'

Indeed, Encrid understood the situation clearly.

The ferryman had offered him an easy way to get through ‘today’. He pondered the words, and the more he did, the more he felt the desire to follow them.

"Is it really necessary to go this far?"

Krais's slightly altered voice echoed in his mind. The ferryman’s words were true. Desire and longing combined with reason to point to a single path.

But why at this moment?

"Maybe I’ll acknowledge you later. If I become famous for making potions, I might even give you one, so you better stay on my good side."

He recalled the image of a small child standing with hands on hips, chin raised, chattering away.

Originally, rejection should only react to the same type of pressure—whether it’s from willpower, a skill like Intimidation, or the inherent fear that monsters instill in humans.

Encrid instinctively knew this but muttered to himself.

'I refuse.'

Yet, in his mind, the 'easy path' kept echoing. Again and again, reason told him it was the right choice. His instincts agreed.

Despite limping, Encrid stepped forward to the front lines.

"…You're going out again today?"

A soldier standing beside him asked.

Encrid’s face was covered with small cuts.

"I’ll be out again tomorrow too."

As he answered, Encrid threw off his leather helmet. Helmets narrowed his vision and dulled some of his senses. This time, he would thrust his sword before the spell could be cast.

‘By the quickest path.’

He traced the path and visualized the movement that would execute it.

A breeze brushed against his cheek.

Though it was daytime, the sky was dark, and the wind that touched his face was a biting cold wind.

Through the sharp, cold air, the scent of the battlefield pierced his nose.

Blood, steel, excrement, fear, terror, excitement, tension.

Everything was interpreted by the brain through the smell, merging the senses divided by the five into one, opening the Gate of the Sixth Sense.

As the Focus Point activated, his brain ignited, making everything on the battlefield appear to move in slow motion.

Soon, a child came running. He blocked out the surrounding sounds.

No need to listen.

He focused solely on the child, ignoring everything else.

No need to see anything else.

All his senses—sight, hearing, smell, touch—blurred together to form a single line.

Point to point.

‘I am also just a point.’

He perceived himself as a point and saw the path the child was running as another point.

The shortest line connecting those two points.

His right knee bent deeply, then extended. Even without 'Will' infused, his thighs, strengthened through intense training, propelled Encrid’s body forward at an astonishing speed.

At the same time, he thrust the sword in his left hand forward.

To the observing soldier, the blade appeared before the human body did.

The blue-tinged blade moved faster than an arrow. That’s how it seemed to the soldier watching.

Encrid faced today more swiftly than any other day.

He saw the child’s face. He saw the eyes. He saw the nose. He saw the mouth.

The face of a child who had dreamed of becoming an herbalist, already dead, overlapped with the child's face in front of him.

The sword struck toward the child’s shoulder. With a delicate adjustment, it cut the strap on the shoulder. The scroll hanging from the child’s chest, now cut in half, wavered as it spilled light.

It was a failure.

*    *    *

“You are foolish.”

The ferryman's voice was flat, devoid of any emotion.

Encrid did not respond. He acted as he had before. He repeated the same ‘today’.

When does a person despair?

If you’re told from the start that something is impossible, you might actually take it calmly.

You accept it. You recognize it as the end.

But what if it seems within reach? What if you almost touch it, but don’t?

Despair arrives at that moment.

And if, at that moment, someone shows you a shortcut, subtly hints at an easier way?

The ferryman, now changed as he was, felt puzzled by this being he had encountered for the first time.

Why doesn’t this human give up?

Why doesn’t this human feel despair?

Why, how can he be like this?

Doubt crept into the ferryman's mind, and that doubt led him to make a second offer.

It was after eighty-six iterations of ‘today’ had passed.

“Even if you regret it, it will be too late.”

Encrid tilted his head at the sudden remark.

Expressing such emotions in the realm of the mind?

It was surprising, but considering how many surprising things this human had done, this was no longer shocking.

“But I am generous.”

“Generous?”

The fact that he responded with a counter question, even adding emphasis, showed how firm his resolve and will were.

In this world of the mind, he spoke not with his body, but with his will.

His manner of speaking and attitude were utterly irreverent, but that didn’t matter.

The ferryman already knew this and realized that if he played along with such antics, he would only make a fool of himself. So he chose to ignore it and continued with what he had to say.

"I’ll give you one more chance."

"Again?"

Even so, that response was grating to hear. The way Encrid tilted his head and furrowed his brow, as if mocking, made it all the more irritating.

However, the ferryman had long since transcended being human, so he remained composed.

If he were still living in the physical world, he would have cursed aloud.

But he was different from such humans, wasn’t he?

"Don’t let the entity behind the wall get closer. Make it cross the river before it reaches you."

The ferryman maintained his aura, while Encrid, still in the same stance as before, questioned again.

"The river?"

The ferryman took a breath, something he usually didn’t need to do. A deep one. Then he dismissed his opponent.

Only after Encrid disappeared from the realm of the mind did the ferryman allow his true feelings to show.

“Bastard.”

It was a brief, but strong and clear expression of his will.

Even though he had tried to subtly manipulate Encrid and plant his intentions in the man's mind…

‘The bastard will act on his own.’

The ferryman had a premonition that Encrid would betray his intentions.

And realizing this, the ferryman unknowingly let out a chuckle.

“Heh.”

It was the first genuine emotion he had shown since becoming a ferryman.

A laugh that was half exasperation, half amusement.

*  *  *

‘He keeps spouting nonsense. Is he bored?’

Encrid, who usually ignored all paths and bulldozed through everything, of course, dismissed this latest offer without a second thought.

His mind was consumed by a single thought.

‘Can it be done any faster?’

He connected the dots, his brain burning with focus, feeling as if his eyes would pop out.

And yet, it was still a failure.

So what is speed?

He thought he had seen many swords that embodied the essence of speed—those that were precise, quick, and sharp.

The answer came suddenly and easily.

"My hand wasn’t the fastest when I used to pickpocket. But I was the best. My hands were a bit slower, but I was quick to read the situation. I just had to strike when the other person wasn’t looking. Trying to win by sheer speed alone is something only fools do, right?"

These words were from Krais, who happened to pass by after Encrid had exchanged the fastest sword strikes with Ragna and discussed the preemptive techniques of Valaf-Style Martial Arts with Audin.

It was a trivial statement, and Krais didn’t even mean much by it.

In fact, what he said next was likely his real point.

"Our enemy knows us well. It’s like trying to pickpocket Krona while they’re staring right at us.”

He was saying that the situation was dire and they needed an unpredictable element, but Encrid didn’t respond.

No, he couldn’t respond.

Krais's words struck Encrid’s mind like lightning.

‘From outside the range of perception.’

Speed is relative.

No matter how fast you are, if your intentions are visible, it’s slow.

If the enemy knows your intentions, they will prepare accordingly.

"Hey, you’re doing it again? Can’t you hear me? Hey? Enki, you bastard!"

Krais waved his hand and jumped up and down in front of him, but Encrid didn’t hear him.

Encrid had sunk deep into his own world. His mouth hung half-open, and drool dripped down.

Even so, his thoughts didn’t stop.

"Enough."

Ragna pulled Krais away.

Encrid was in the process of breaking through something that had been confining his thoughts.

The enemy’s intentions and his own.

A human can convey meaning with just a gesture.

The art of distracting an opponent’s attention with hand movements developed from this. Magic tricks performed with sleight of hand work in the same way.

It’s a skill easily seen in gambling dens.

Such is the nature of intent.

‘Deceive.’

One can deceive an opponent with mere intent.

Speed is acting outside the opponent's perception.

Is this a battle of speed fought right in front of the enemy’s eyes?

No, at least Encrid didn’t see it that way. This time, the wall was about whether he could save the child or not.

He had made up his mind about that.

Therefore, what he needed was an Illusionary Sword or a Phantom Sword.

The Valen Mercenary Sword Technique had countless such techniques.

‘Ah.’

A realization dawned on him. Lightning struck his mind repeatedly.

What is speed?

It's executing an action outside the opponent's perception.

It's ending the fight without even showing the opponent a fast sword.

The image of Jaxon’s deathless thrust flashed through his mind.

He added something new.

‘The Sense of Evasion relies on instinct.’

The Sense of Evasion avoids anything that falls within the realm of the sixth sense. What if he added intent on top of that?

What if he gave direction to that instinct?

This was a path he hadn’t seen before.

It felt just within reach, and that’s why. That’s why he had been fixated on speed.

But no, there isn’t just one path.

‘And if it’s also absolutely fast, even better.’

They say if you chase two rabbits, you won’t catch either.

But the experiences he’d accumulated, his left arm honed through mistakes, and his speed—all these things now showed him the path where both rabbits were running. It felt like he could catch them both.

Above all, the previous training with Jaxon had been helpful.

Hadn’t he trained in using the Sense of Evasion at close range?

What was the purpose of dodging stones during that training?

Every question must have an intent, and every training session was a process leading to the result.

For Encrid, the result was singular.

‘Intent over instinct.’

The Sense of Evasion is a celebration of instinct. It makes the body react through the sixth sense and intuition.

That’s why it’s called the Sense of Evasion. It’s a skill born from the instinct to protect one’s body.

Encrid twisted that skill.

‘Imbue it with intent.’

It was a concept that could just as well be called the Sense of Attack.

Clang.

The psychological shackles the ferryman had implanted were shattered.

The wall that seemed so easily within reach, yet insurmountable.

And then, the offer that was given at that moment.

Everything is a trap. Everything is a prison that confines him.

But Encrid didn’t even set foot near the prison.

He ignored the offer and found a new path on his own.

‘Ah.’

At the end of this realization, before he knew it, a new day had come, and the battlefield awaited him.

“Again today…”

“What doesn’t kill me…”

He was at the forefront of the battlefield, cutting off the words of the soldier who asked the same question every day beside him.

The soldier blankly met his gaze and finished the sentence.

“…only makes me stronger.”

In reality, it’s the pain that doesn’t kill you that makes you stronger.

But the current phrase resonated more with him.

Encrid pushed through the biting wind and stepped forward.

From the other side of the battlefield, a child wrapped in scrolls came running towards him.
content_cn:
黑暗的河面上，一叶扁舟静静漂浮。紫色的提灯孤零零地立在船头，光芒微弱，仅能照亮自己的双手。

虽是熟悉的场景，今日却格外阴森。连摆渡人的语调都比往日沉重，字字敲在心头，震得人神魂发颤。

恩克里德听到某个有趣的词，忍不住开口：

“一条路？”

他不是该享受被逼至绝境的痛苦吗？为何突然指点起迷津？

“退后，看着。若你避开，路自会显现。”

往常的“今日”，本应是忍耐与重生的开端。换言之，逃避绝非面对明日的方式。

可此刻，竟有人叫他回避？

摆渡人的声音愈发沉厚：

“避开。”

那哀鸣般的嗓音贯穿恩克里德的全身，仿佛要撕裂他的五脏六腑。

并无痛楚，这只是一种感觉——不，此处是心象领域，连感觉都算不上。这是精神层面的冲击。

重要的不是声音带来的影响，而是更深层的东西。

恩克里德不怀疑摆渡人，也不质疑其目的。摆渡人的职责本就是将他困在“今日”。

或许正因如此，他才恍然明白。

“恶魔总披着天使的外衣。”

奥丁每日诵读的圣典文句，忽然浮现脑海。

“避开。”

摆渡人仍在撕扯恩克里德的意识，搅乱他的思绪，却继续说着。

话音落下，黑暗的河流骤然退去。恩克里德又一次睁开了眼——这体验颇为诡异，仿佛先前已睁过一次——新的一天已然来临。

摆渡人的话语比以往任何一次都清晰地烙印在他心中。寻常梦境总会淡成模糊记忆，这次却不然。简直像被洗了脑。

‘逃吧。回头。只要舍弃一个孩子，今日便能轻松度过。那样便够了。’

的确，恩克里德对局势一清二楚。

摆渡人为他指了条轻松度过“今日”的捷径。他反复咀嚼那些话，越想，越有种遵从的冲动。

“真有必要做到这地步吗？”

克莱斯那略带异样的嗓音在脑中回响。摆渡人说得没错，欲望与渴求交织着理性，共同指向唯一的路。

可为何偏偏是此刻？

“说不定我以后会认可你。要是因炼药出了名，或许还能赏你一瓶，所以最好乖乖讨好我。”

他想起那孩子双手叉腰、昂着下巴喋喋不休的模样。

原本，“抗拒”只应对同类的压力起反应——无论是意志力、威吓这类技能，还是怪物天生带给人的恐惧。

恩克里德本能地知晓这点，却喃喃自语：

‘我拒绝。’

然而脑中，“捷径”仍在不断回响。理性一次次告诉他这是正确选择，本能也表示赞同。

尽管步履蹒跚，恩克里德仍迈步走向前线。

“……今天又要出去？”

身旁的士兵问道。

恩克里德脸上布满细碎伤口。

“明天也会出去。”

他答着，一把扯下皮盔。头盔会窄化视野、钝化感官。这一次，他要在咒术施放前便刺出剑刃。

‘以最快的路径。’

他描摹轨迹，在脑中演练动作。

微风拂过脸颊。

虽是白昼，天色却晦暗。拂面的风带着刺骨寒意。

凛冽寒气中，战场的气味钻入鼻腔。

血、铁、秽物、恐惧、惊怖、兴奋、紧绷。

一切经由气味被大脑解读，将五感熔铸为一，推开第六感之门。

专注点激活，脑海如燃火，战场上的一切仿佛都慢了下来。

很快，一个孩子奔来。他屏蔽了周遭杂音。

无需去听。

他只盯着那孩子，其余一概不理。

无需再看其他。

所有感官——视觉、听觉、嗅觉、触觉——模糊交融，化作一条线。

点与点相连。

‘我也只是一个点。’

他将自身视为一点，孩子奔跑的轨迹视为另一点。

连接两点最短的线。

右膝深屈，继而蹬直。即便未灌注“意志”，经严酷锻炼而强健的大腿仍爆发出惊人推力，推动恩克里德的身躯疾射向前。

同时，左手剑疾刺而出。

在旁观的士兵眼中，剑锋竟比人身先至。

泛着蓝芒的刃，快得胜过箭矢——至少那士兵看来如此。

恩克里德以比以往任何一日都更快的速度，迎向今日。

他看见孩子的脸。看见眼睛。看见鼻子。看见嘴。

那张梦想成为草药师、却已死去的孩子的面容，与眼前的孩子重叠。

剑锋斩向孩子肩头。微调角度，削断肩带。挂在孩子胸前的卷轴应声断为两半，光芒流泻，摇曳不定。

失败了。

*    *    *

“你真愚蠢。”

摆渡人的声音平板，毫无情绪。

恩克里德没有回应。他如之前一般行动，重复着同一个“今日”。

人何时会绝望？

若一开始便被告知绝无可能，或许反而能坦然接受。

认命。视之为终局。

但若看似触手可及呢？若几乎碰触，却终究差之毫厘？

绝望便在那刻降临。

而若此时，有人为你指出一条捷径，隐晦暗示更轻松的路？

如今的摆渡人，对这初次相遇的存在感到不解。

为何这人不放弃？

为何这人不绝望？

为何——他怎能如此？

疑虑渗入摆渡人心头，促使他提出第二次交易。

那是在“今日”重复第八十六次之后。

“即便后悔，也为时已晚。”

恩克里德对这突兀之言歪了歪头。

在心象领域流露情绪？

虽令人意外，但想到此人已做出诸多惊人之举，这倒也不足为奇。

“但我很慷慨。”

“慷慨？”

他竟反问，甚至加重语气，足见其心志之坚。

在这心象世界，他并非以身体，而是以意志发声。

那说话的态度姿态可谓十足不敬，但这已不重要。

摆渡人早已知晓，若配合这等把戏只会自取其辱，故而选择无视，继续陈述：

“我再给你一次机会。”

“又来？”

即便如此，这回应仍刺耳得很。恩克里德歪头蹙眉，那嘲弄般的神态更令人火大。

然而摆渡人早已超脱人身，故仍从容。

若尚存于世，他早破口大骂。

但他与那般凡人不同，不是吗？

“莫让墙后的存在靠近。令其在触及你之前渡河。”

摆渡人维持着气场，恩克里德却仍保持先前的姿态，再度发问：

“河？”

摆渡人吸了口气——这本非必需。深深一口。随即挥退对手。

直至恩克里德彻底离开心象领域，摆渡人才容许真情流露。

“混账。”

简短，却强烈而清晰地传达其意志。

尽管他已巧妙操纵，将意图植入对方心神……

‘这厮会自行其是。’

摆渡人预感到恩克里德将违背其意。

意识到这点，他不自觉轻笑出声。

“呵。”

这是他成为摆渡人以来，首度流露的真实情绪。

那笑声半是恼火，半是玩味。

*  *  *

‘尽说些胡话。是闲得发慌么？’

向来无视一切路径、横冲直撞的恩克里德，自然将这最新提议抛诸脑后。

他心神已被唯一念头占据：

‘还能更快吗？’

连点成线，专注灼烧脑海，眼球几欲迸出。

然而，仍是失败。

那么，速度究竟是什么？

他以为自己见过诸多体现速度精髓的剑——精准、迅疾、锋锐。

答案来得突然而轻易。

“我以前扒窃时，手并非最快。但我却是最厉害的。手稍慢些，但我擅察形势，只需趁对方不备时出手。妄想单凭速度取胜，那是蠢货所为，对吧？”

这话是克莱斯说的——就在恩克里德与拉格纳比拼最快剑击、又与奥丁探讨瓦拉夫流先发制人之术之后，克莱斯恰巧路过时所言。

本是随口一句，克莱斯也未深意。

事实上，他接下来说的才是重点：

“敌人太了解我们了。这好比在克朗人瞪着眼时扒他们的口袋。”

他是说局势危急，需引入变数，但恩克里德未答。

不，是无法作答。

克莱斯的话如惊雷劈入恩克里德脑海。

‘在感知范围之外。’

速度是相对的。

再快，若意图暴露，便是慢。

若敌知你意图，自会早做防备。

“喂，又来了？听不见吗？嘿！恩基，你这混蛋！”

克莱斯在眼前挥手蹦跳，恩克里德却充耳不闻。

他已沉入自己的世界。半张着嘴，涎水淌下。

即便如此，思绪未停。

“够了。”

拉格纳将克莱斯拉开。

恩克里德正冲破某种长久束缚思维的枷锁。

敌之意图，与己之意图。

人仅凭姿态便可传递心意。

以手势分散注意的技巧便由此衍生。魔术师的手法亦是同理。

这在赌坊中司空见惯。

意图的本质便是如此。

‘欺骗。’

仅凭意图便可欺敌。

速度，便是在对手感知之外行动。

这真是敌人眼前的速度之争吗？

不，至少恩克里德不这么看。这一次，高墙在于能否救下孩子。

对此，他心意已决。

故而，他需要的乃是虚招或幻剑。

瓦伦佣兵剑术中，此类技法数不胜数。

‘啊。’

灵光乍现。惊雷连连劈落脑海。

何谓速度？

是在对手感知之外完成动作。

是未让对手见识快剑，便已终结战斗。

贾克森那“不死突刺”的景象闪过脑海。

他添上新的一笔：

‘闪避之感，依凭本能。’

闪避之感会避开一切落入第六感范畴之物。若在此基础上附加意图呢？

若为那本能赋予方向呢？

这是一条前所未见之路。

正因看似触手可及，他才一直执迷于速度。

但不，路非仅一条。

‘若同时绝对迅疾，自然更佳。’

常言道，追二兔者不得其一。

可他累积的经验、在错误中磨砺的左臂、他的速度——这一切如今为他指明二兔同奔之径。仿佛两者皆可擒获。

更重要的是，先前与贾克森的对练大有裨益。

他不是练过近距离运用闪避之感吗？

那时躲避飞石，目的何在？

每个问题必含意图，每次训练皆导向结果。

于恩克里德而言，结果唯一。

‘以意图驾驭本能。’

闪避之感是本能的礼赞。它借第六感与直觉令身体反应。

故而得名。这是护卫己身的本能所生之技。

恩克里德将之扭转。

‘为其灌注意图。’

这概念，或可称为“攻击之感”。

锵。

摆渡人植入的心锁应声碎裂。

那看似近在咫尺、却难以逾越的高墙。

以及墙前递来的交易。

一切皆是陷阱。一切皆是囚笼。

但恩克里德连笼边都未靠近。

他无视交易，独自寻得新路。

‘啊。’

悟透此理时，不知不觉，新日已临，战场在望。

“今天又……”

“杀不死我的……”

他立于战线最前，截断了身旁每日重复同一问的士兵的话头。

士兵茫然迎上他的目光，接完下半句：

“……只会让我更强。”

事实上，杀不死你的痛苦令你强大。

但此刻这句话更合他心境。

恩克里德破开刺骨寒风，踏步向前。

战场彼端，一个浑身缠满卷轴的孩子，正朝他奔来。
content_cn_raw:
场景发生在一条黑暗的河流上，一艘小渡船上。一盏紫色的灯静静地立着，独自闪耀，但它的光芒几乎没有蔓延到附近。只要能看到自己的双手就足够了。

尽管是熟悉的环境，但今天却感觉比平时更加​​诡异。就连摆渡人的语气也更加沉重，震彻心扉，震动心灵。

安克里德对所讲内容中特别有趣的部分很感兴趣，问道。

“一条路？”

他不是应该享受被困在墙边的痛苦吗？那么为什么要给建议呢？

“退后观察。如果你躲避，道路就会打开。”

通常，今天是忍受并重新开始的一天。换句话说，逃避并不是面对明天的方法。

但现在，却被告知要回避？

摆渡人的声音变得更加沉重。

“逃避。”

哀嚎的声音贯穿了恩克里德的整个身体，仿佛要撕裂他的五脏六腑。

没有疼痛，只是感觉的问题。不，这是心灵领域，所以连感觉都算不上。这是一个精神问题。

重要的不是声音的效果，而是更深层次的东西。

安克里德并不怀疑摆渡人。他也没有质疑他的目的。摆渡人的作用就是让他留在“今天”。

也许这就是他明白的原因。

“恶魔总是披着天使的外衣而来。”

突然，奥丁每天背诵的圣典中的一句话浮现在脑海中。

“逃避。”

摆渡人仍在撕裂恩克里德的内心，扰乱他的思绪，继续说话。

说完，黑暗的河流突然退去，安克里德发现自己再次睁开了眼睛，这是一种奇怪的经历，就好像他以前已经睁开过一次一样，他面临着新的一天。

摆渡人的话在他脑海中更加清晰地萦绕着。与通常的梦境不同的是，梦境会消失在模糊的记忆中，而这个梦却没有。感觉自己好像被洗脑了一样。

'逃跑。折回。只要抛弃一个孩子，这一天你就能轻松度过。这就足够了。

确实，恩克里德很清楚现在的情况。

摆渡人为他提供了一种轻松度过“今天”的方法。他思考着这句话，越思考，他就越想跟着它们走。

“真的有必要走到这一步吗？”

克莱斯略微改变的声音在他脑海中回响。摆渡人的话是真的。欲望和渴望与理性相结合，指向一条单一的道路。

但为什么是在这个时候呢？

“也许以后我会认出你的。如果我因制作魔药而出名，我什至可能会给你一个，所以你最好站在我这边。”

他回忆起一个小孩子双手叉腰站着、扬起下巴、喋喋不休的画面。

最初，拒绝只应该对同一类型的压力做出反应——无论是来自意志力、恐吓等技能，还是怪物灌输给人类的固有恐惧。

安克里德本能地知道这一点，但他自言自语道。

“我拒绝。”

然而，在他的脑海里，“简单的道路”一直在回响。理智一次又一次告诉他，这是正确的选择。他的直觉同意了。

尽管一瘸一拐，恩克里德还是挺身而出，奔赴前线。

“……今天又要出去吗？”

站在他身边的一名士兵问道。

安克里德的脸上布满了小伤口。

“明天我也会出去。”

当他回答时，恩克里德扔掉了他的皮头盔。头盔缩小了他的视野，并削弱了他的一些感官。这一次，他会在咒语施展之前刺出剑。

“走最快的路。”

他追踪路径并想象执行该路径的动作。

微风拂过他的脸颊。

虽然是白天，但天色却很暗，吹到脸上的风是刺骨的寒风。

凛冽的空气中，战场的气息刺入他的鼻腔。

鲜血、钢铁、粪便、恐惧、恐怖、兴奋、紧张。

一切都被大脑通过气味解读，将五分的感官合而为一，打开了第六感之门。

随着焦点的启动，他的大脑被点燃，让战场上的一切都像是慢动作一样移动。

不久，一个小孩跑了过来。他屏蔽了周围的声音。

无需听。

他只顾着孩子，忽略了其他一切。

无需看到其他任何东西。

他所有的感官——视觉、听觉、嗅觉、触觉——模糊在一起，形成一条线。

点对点。

“我也只是一个点。”

他将自己视为一个点，将孩子奔跑的路径视为另一个点。

The shortest line connecting those two points.

His right knee bent deeply, then extended.即使没有注入‘意志’，经过高强度训练而变得强壮的大腿，也推动着恩克里德的身体以惊人的速度前进。

与此同时，他将左手的剑向前刺出。

对于观察的士兵来说，刀刃先于人体出现。

蓝色的刀刃移动得比箭还要快。 That’s how it seemed to the soldier watching.

安克里德面对今天的速度比任何一天都快。

He saw the child’s face.他看到了眼睛。他看到了鼻子。他看到了嘴。

一个梦想成为草药师的孩子的脸，已经死了，和眼前孩子的脸重叠在一起。

The sword struck toward the child’s shoulder.经过微妙的调整，它剪断了肩上的带子。挂在孩子胸口的卷轴现在被切成两半，随着光芒的溢出而摇曳。

这是一次失败。

*    *    *

“你真是个傻子。”

摆渡人的声音平淡，没有任何感情。

安克里德没有回应。他的表现和以前一样。 He repeated the same ‘today’.

When does a person despair?

如果你从一开始就被告知某件事是不可能的，你实际上可能会冷静地对待它。

你接受它。你将其视为结局。

但如果它看起来触手可及怎么办？ What if you almost touch it, but don’t?

绝望就在那一刻降临。

如果在那一刻，有人向你展示了一条捷径，巧妙地暗示了一种更简单的方法？

变了样的摆渡人对第一次遇到的这个生物感到困惑。

为什么这个人类不放弃？

为什么这个人不感到绝望？

Why, how can he be like this?

摆渡人心中生出疑虑，这种疑虑促使他提出第二次提议。

那是在“今天”重复了八十六次之后。

“就算你后悔，也已经晚了。”

听到这突如其来的言论，恩克里德歪了歪头。

在心灵领域表达这样的情感？

这很令人惊讶，但考虑到这个人类做了多少令人惊讶的事情，这已经不再令人震惊了。

“但我很慷慨。”

“慷慨的？”

他反问，甚至强调，足见他的决心和意志有多么坚定。

在这个心灵的世界里，他不是用身体说话，而是用意志说话。

他的说话方式和态度完全不敬，但这并不重要。

摆渡人已经知道了这一点，他意识到如果自己再配合这种滑稽动作，只会出丑。于是他选择无视，继续说自己要说的话。

“我再给你一次机会。”

“再次？”

Even so, that response was grating to hear.安克里德歪着头，皱着眉头，仿佛在嘲笑，这让这一切变得更加令人恼火。

但摆渡人早已超越了人类，所以他依然泰然自若。

如果他还生活在现实世界，他一定会破口大骂。

但他和这样的人类不同，不是吗？

“不要让墙后的实体靠近。让它在到达你之前过河。”

摆渡人保持着气场，而安克里德则依然保持着之前的姿势，再次问道。

“河？”

摆渡人吸了一口气，这是他通常不需要做的事情。很深的一个。然后他驳回了他的对手。

直到安克里德从心灵领域消失之后，摆渡人才露出了自己的真实感受。

“混蛋。”

这是他的意志的简短但强烈而清晰的表达。

尽管他试图巧妙地操纵恩克里德并将自己的意图植入到这个人的脑海中……

“这个混蛋会自己行动。”

摆渡人预感到恩克里德会背叛他的意图。

意识到这一点，摆渡人不自觉的轻笑了一声。

“呵呵。”

这是他成为摆渡人以来，第一次流露出真正的情感。

笑声一半是恼怒，一半是有趣。

*  *  *

‘他一直在胡言乱语。他无聊吗？

恩克里德通常会忽略所有路径并推倒一切，当然，他不假思索地拒绝了最新的提议。

他的大脑被一个念头吞噬了。

“能再快点吗？”

他把这些点联系起来，他的大脑因专注而燃烧，感觉自己的眼睛都要掉出来了。

然而，它仍然是失败的。

那么什么是速度呢？

他认为他见过很多体现速度本质的剑——精准、快速、锋利。

答案来得突然而轻松。

“以前扒手的时候，我的手不是最快的，但我是最好的。我的手慢了一点，但我很快就能看清形势，只要趁别人不注意的时候出手，光靠速度取胜，那是傻子才会做的事吧？”

这句话是在恩克里德与拉格纳交锋并与奥丁讨论瓦拉夫流先发制人之术之后，恰巧路过的克莱斯说出的。

这只是一句微不足道的话，克莱斯甚至没有什么意思。

事实上，他接下来说的话很可能才是他真正的意思。

“我们的敌人很了解我们。这就像在他们盯着我们的时候试图扒窃克朗一样。”

他说情况很严峻，他们需要一个不可预测的因素，但安克里德没有回应。

不，他无法回应。

克莱斯的话如闪电般击中了恩克里德的脑海。

“来自感知范围之外。”

速度是相对的。

无论你的速度有多快，如果你的意图是可见的，那么它就会很慢。

如果敌人知道你的意图，他们就会做出相应的准备。

“嘿，你又来了？你听不到我说话吗？嘿？恩基，你这个混蛋！”

克莱斯挥了挥手，在他面前跳来跳去，但恩克里德没有听到他的声音。

安克里德已经深深陷入了自己的世界。他的嘴半张着，口水滴下来。

即便如此，他的思绪却没有停止。

“足够的。”

拉格纳拉开克莱斯。

安克里德正在突破一些一直限制他思想的东西。

敌人的意图和他自己的意图。

人类只需一个手势就可以传达意义。

通过手势分散对手注意力的艺术由此发展而来。魔术表演也以同样的方式进行。

这是在赌场里很容易看到的技巧。

这就是意图的本质。

“欺骗。”

一个人可以仅凭意图欺骗对手。

速度是在对手感知之外的行动。

这是在敌人眼前进行的速度战吗？

不，至少安克里德不这么认为。这一次，墙是关于他能否救回孩子的。

他已经下定决心了。

所以，他需要的是一把幻剑或者幻剑。

瓦伦佣兵剑法中，这样的技巧数不胜数。

“啊。”

他恍然大悟。闪电一次又一次地击中他的脑海。

什么是速度？

它正在执行对手感知之外的行动。

甚至没有向对手展示快剑就结束了战斗。

贾克森的不死刺击的画面在他脑海中闪过。

他添加了一些新东西。

“逃避之感依赖于本能。”

躲避之感会避免任何属于第六感范围的事情。如果他在此基础上加上意图呢？

如果他引导这种本能呢？

这是一条他从未见过的路。

感觉触手可及，这就是原因。这就是为什么他一直专注于速度。

但不，并不只有一条路。

“如果它也绝对快，那就更好了。”

他们说，如果你追两只兔子，你一只也抓不到。

但他所积累的经验、他在错误中磨练的左臂以及他的速度——所有这些现在都向他展示了两只兔子正在奔跑的道路。感觉他能抓住他们两个。

最重要的是，之前与 贾克森 的训练很有帮助。

他不是训练过近距离使用闪避之感吗？

那次训练中躲避石头的目的是什么？

每一个问题都必须有一个意图，每一次培训都是一个导致结果的过程。

对于 Encrid 来说，结果是非凡的。

“意图胜过本能。”

逃避感是对本能的庆祝。它使身体通过第六感和直觉做出反应。

这就是为什么它被称为逃避感。这是一种出于保护身体本能而生的技能。

Encrid 扭曲了这个技能。

“用意图来灌输它。”

这是一个可以称为攻击感的概念。

铛。

摆渡人所植入的心理枷锁被打破了。

这堵墙看似触手可及，却又难以逾越。

然后，就是当时给出的报价。

一切都是陷阱。一切都是一座囚禁他的监狱。

但安克里德甚至没有踏足监狱附近一步。

他无视了这个提议，自己找到了一条新路。

“啊。”

当他意识到这一点时，不知不觉间，新的一天已经到来，战场在等待着他。

“今天又来了……”

“什么杀不了我……”

他站在战场最前线，打断了身边每天问同样问题的士兵的话。

士兵茫然地与他对视，说完这句话。

“……只会让我变得更强。”

事实上，那些杀不死你的痛苦会让你变得更坚强。

但现在这句话更能引起他的共鸣。

安克里德推开刺骨的寒风，向前走去。

战场的另一边，一个裹着卷轴的小孩朝他跑来。
